channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Cyberpunk 2077 PS5 Pro Enhanced Update Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Cyberpunk 2077’s PS5 Pro Enhanced update arrives tomorrow on April 8, 2026, and here’s what’s new.

CD Projekt Red has been absolutely smashing it with the Cyberpunk 2077 franchise, with the newly announced Trading Card Game already setting records at Kickstarter. The work on the game is also continuing as we wait for the sequel, and now the upgrade we thought would never arrive has finally been released tomorrow.

Cyberpunk 2077 Gets 3 Distinct Modes on PS5 Pro

The latest blog post provides us with details on the PS5 Pro Enhanced update for Cyberpunk 2077. Firstly, PlayStation Super Spectral Resolution has been used on each pixel to upscale every inch of Night City. Coming to Ray Tracing, the developers have “implemented BVH8 (8-way Bounding Volume Hierarchy) support to implement ray-traced lighting, shadows, and reflections, bringing the lighting even closer to our artistic vision for this world.”

Cyberpunk 2077 on PS5 Pro gets three distinct modes. First is Ray Tracing Pro, which offers all available ray-tracing enhancements and the most visually advanced version of Cyberpunk 2077 on the console. It will include traced reflections, ambient occlusion, skylight, shadows, and emissive lighting, and provide 40 FPS on variable refresh rate-enabled displays and 30 FPS for others.

Then we have Performance mode for players looking for the most fluid experience. VRR Displays will support up to 90 FPS with high image fidelity for Cyberpunk 2077 on PS5 Pro. The last mode is Ray Tracing, which sits between the two modes, focusing on visuals and performance. IT will enable select ray tracing enhancements and provide a frame rate of 60FPS.

In other news, CD Projekt Red has stated that they have “no plans” to make new DLCs or expansions for Cyberpunk 2077. What are your thoughts on Cyberpunk 2077’s PS5 Pro upgrades? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the The Legend of Zelda movie bringing in a Bafta-winning composer. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: The Legend of Zelda Movie Brings in Bafta-Winning Composer
item.content:encoded:
The composer for the upcoming The Legend of Zelda movie has been confirmed: Bafta-winning composer John Paesano.

The Legend of Zelda movie has been progressing well, as it seems to have started filming in November 2025. Since then, several videos featuring the movie sets have leaked online. Now, new details about the upcoming adaptation have been revealed, including the stunt doubles and the soundtrack composer.

The Legend of Zelda Movie’s Soundtrack Will be Composed by John Paesano

The news about the composer of The Legend of Zelda movie comes from an X post (formerly Twitter) by MikeOdysseyYT and the movie’s IMDb page. John Paesano will be coming on as the soundtrack composer. He will be advised by Koji Kondo, known for composing for video game franchises such as The Legend of Zelda, Super Mario, and more.

Paesano has composed tracks for Insomniac’s Marvel’s Spider-Man games, Detroit: Become Human, Mass Effect Andromeda, and more. He won a Bafta for his work on Miles Morales and has worked on films such as The Maze Runner, Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes, and more.

The tweet also reveals the stunt doubles for Link and Zelda in the upcoming film, with Joel Abadal stepping in for Benjamin Evan Ainsworth and Jacqueline Lee Geurts for Bo Bragason. Mike also highlights that the list of crew has been revealed on the IMDB page, with only the cast left to be announced, which is speculated to be revealed at the franchise’s 40th Anniversary celebrations in June.

In other news, The Legend of Zelda’s live-action Link and Zelda have been officially revealed in costume by Nintendo. Additionally, a Zelda-themed limited-edition Nintendo Switch 2 console is reportedly in the works. What are your thoughts on the composer of The Legend of Zelda movie? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Frostpunk 2 Breach of Trust DLC release date and other details. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: 7 New Fortnite Game Modes Have Leaked
item.content:encoded:
If you’re worried about what the future of Fortnite looks like and if it’ll have enough fun new content to play, leaks suggest Epic Games is cooking up more than a few new game modes to keep you busy.

In one fell swoop, Epic declared that Fortnite was shutting down several popular game modes for good. You’ll never see the permanent back of Zero Build or traditional battle royale, but there will always be question marks over other modes. That said, if content is removed, there’s a good chance it’ll be replaced.

Fortnite Getting Many New Game Modes

According to @Loolo_WRLD: “Epic is working on a TON of new modes.” To be more exact, Epic is working on seven new game modes.

The popular leaker has not only come up with an exact figure but revealed the names of each one and what to expect from them:

  • “WickedSmoke: This will be the next “Social-First” mode (like Delulu)!
  • UnableRoman: This is the previously leaked TDM (Team Deathmatch) mode!
  • Rivalry: This is the Mech-Based Lego mode.
  • CurioBox: This is the previously leaked new Lego mode!
  • MatchMist: New Reload related mode/map
  • BabyCorgi: New mode likely related to Ballistic
  • Bulldog and Husky: Related to Disney mode!”

From Team Deathmatch to LEGO additions, there should be plenty to look forward to then if the leaks are accurate.

There’s no indication as to when any of this potential content could arrive, though, and as with everything, unless it’s official, it doesn’t mean it’s happening. So stay tuned for further Fortnite leaks and updates.

Which mode piques your interest the most? With several of Fortnite’s beloved game modes set to depart in 2026, what would you like to see Epic add in time? Offer some suggestions and wishlist answers through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out even more IG news: Sony beta testing a new PlayStation 5 UI, the main protagonist in Rockstar Games’ canceled Agent reportedly being revealed, and the Pokémon Champions mobile release period being announced.
